name: Path Equivalence: '/./' (Single Dot Directory)
description: The product does not properly handle '/./' (single dot directory) sequences in paths, which resolve to the current directory and are effectively no-ops, allowing attackers to bypass path-based access controls that match the literal '/./' form but not the canonical resolved form.
detection_pattern: INGRESS node provides a path containing '/./' sequences that bypasses CONTROL[path_check] because the check matches the literal form while the OS resolves '/./' to '/' at STORAGE[file_access], creating a check-vs-use mismatch
